next_steps: Complete definitive pathological workup (immunohistochemistry for CD20, CD3, CD68, GFAP, and EBER-ISH)
Initiate empiric coverage for Listeria (ampicillin) and Nocardia (trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole) while awaiting microbiology
Lumbar puncture for CSF analysis (cytology, flow cytometry, protein, glucose, and PCR for EBV, JC virus, Toxoplasma, and Tropheryma whipplei)
Consult infectious disease for management of opportunistic infections in an immunosuppressed patient
Hold dexamethasone if possible until a firm diagnosis is made to avoid masking or lysis of potential lymphoma
Review history for signs of systemic Nocardiosis (e.g., skin, lung, or joint involvement)
